pr.title: feat(react-mcp): new package for user-managed MCP servers

slices.title: OAuth state, callback routing, token storage, and refresh
slices.why: This is the new trust boundary: createOAuthProvider.ts owns OAuth state, PKCE, DCR and token refresh, useMcpOAuthCallback.tsx redeems callback codes, and the storage resources persist tokens and PKCE verifiers.

hunks.reason: PKCE, DCR, state encoding, token refresh and persisted auth state
OAuth callback id routing and code redemption

evidence: packages/react-mcp/src/auth/createOAuthProvider.ts: verify encode/decode state, PKCE verifier persistence, DCR client persistence, and refresh token writes.
packages/react-mcp/src/hooks/useMcpOAuthCallback.tsx: check callback is idempotent under React Strict Mode so code redemption cannot run twice.
packages/react-mcp/src/resources/storage/McpLocalStorage.ts: localStorage stores tokens in plaintext; docs need to keep production escape hatch via McpCustomStorage.
acceptConditions: Accept if state round-trip routes the callback to exactly one server id and invalid/mismatched state fails closed.
Accept if token, verifier, client, and refresh persistence are intentionally scoped and docs call out plaintext localStorage risk.
commentConditions: Comment if useMcpOAuthCallback.tsx can redeem the same code twice under Strict Mode remounts.
Comment if refresh-token updates or clearAuthState can leave stale plaintext tokens in localStorage.

inlineComments.body: Verify this callback cannot redeem the same OAuth code twice during React Strict Mode double effect execution.

slices.title: Server connect/auth/failure/disconnect transport lifecycle
slices.why: McpServerResource.ts owns StreamableHTTP transport creation, authRequired transitions, connect failures, tool refresh, disconnect, remove, and cleanup. The server primitives expose those lifecycle paths to users.

hunks.reason: Connect, authRequired, failure, disconnect, remove and cleanup paths

evidence: packages/react-mcp/src/resources/McpServerResource.ts: check every connect/authRequired/failure/disconnect/remove path closes the active transport and client.
packages/react-mcp/src/primitives/server/McpServerOAuthLink.tsx: lifecycle state controls whether an OAuth URL is exposed.
packages/react-mcp/src/primitives/server/McpServerTools.tsx: tool list rendering depends on connected state and stable tool identity.
acceptConditions: Accept if transport cleanup runs on disconnect, failed connect, authRequired replacement, and remove.
Accept if buttons and OAuthLink only render in lifecycle states where the action is valid.
commentConditions: Comment if a failed or removed server can leave an active StreamableHTTP transport or MCP client alive.
Comment if authRequired transitions can leak a stale OAuth URL or stale tools.

slices.title: Manager store, hydration, connector lookup, and custom server records
slices.why: McpManagerResource.ts and mcp-scope.ts define the manager/store contract, custom server persistence, hydration, connector ids, and per-server scoping.

hunks.reason: Manager resource hydration, custom server records, and persistence
Store state shape and custom server records

evidence: packages/react-mcp/src/resources/McpManagerResource.ts: inspect load/save custom server records and hydration ordering.
packages/react-mcp/src/mcp-scope.ts: verify store fields distinguish preset connectors from user custom server records.
packages/react-mcp/src/context/McpServerByIdProvider.tsx: confirm per-server scope resolution cannot select the wrong server id.
acceptConditions: Accept if custom server add/remove/update operations persist records and hydrate exactly once into the manager store.
Accept if connector and custom-server ids remain stable across storage reloads.
commentConditions: Comment if hydration can overwrite user custom server records or duplicate preset connectors.
Comment if server lookup by index can drift when lists reorder.

slices.title: Tap resources, modelContext tool registration, and public API surface
slices.why: The package exports a Tap/resource surface and modelContext tools contract. Even though McpManagerResource.ts owns the implementation, this slice checks the public API wiring and package-level contract.

hunks.reason: Public API exports for resources, primitives, hooks, and adapters
Package entrypoints and dependencies for Tap/modelContext integration

evidence: packages/react-mcp/src/index.ts: verify public API exports include resource, primitive, hook, storage, and mcpRuntimeToolsToAiSdkTools contracts.
packages/react-mcp/src/resources/McpManagerResource.ts: referenced as evidence for Tap resource and modelContext tool registration behavior.
packages/react-mcp/package.json: check package dependencies expose @assistant-ui/store, @assistant-ui/tap, and MCP SDK assumptions.
acceptConditions: Accept if exported names match the docs/SPEC and no internal-only store details leak as public API.
Accept if modelContext tool registration has a stable collision strategy and does not auto-register outside the mounted store.
commentConditions: Comment if public API names churn from the PR body or docs contract.
Comment if tool names, modelContext registration, or the AI SDK adapter create ambiguous runtime behavior.

slices.title: Add form primitives and render-function list behavior
slices.why: McpAddFormRoot.tsx owns submitted custom-server form state, auth field rendering, validation, and primitive composition. Manager and server list primitives must key render-function lists by stable ids, not array index.

hunks.reason: Custom server form state, validation, submit and cancel behavior
Connector render-function list behavior and key choice

evidence: packages/react-mcp/src/primitives/addForm/McpAddFormRoot.tsx: inspect form validation, auth mode changes, submit side effects, and reset behavior.
packages/react-mcp/src/primitives/manager/McpManagerConnectors.tsx: ensure render-function list keys use stable connector ids, not index.
packages/react-mcp/src/primitives/manager/McpManagerCustomServers.tsx: ensure custom server list keys use stable stored ids, not index.
acceptConditions: Accept if form submission validates name/url/auth fields and uses the manager addCustomServer contract without stale draft state.
Accept if connector/custom/server tool lists render with stable ids instead of index keys.
commentConditions: Comment if McpAddFormRoot.tsx can submit incomplete bearer/OAuth auth records.
Comment if any render-function list is keyed by array index and can remount the wrong scoped server.

slices.title: Docs, examples, package metadata, changeset, and lockfile support
slices.why: Support material should validate the runtime contract but should not outrank OAuth, lifecycle, store, or public API risk.

hunks.reason: Docs describe storage, OAuth callback, custom servers, and support caveats
Example OAuth MCP server exercises the support flow

evidence: apps/docs/content/docs/integrations/tools/react-mcp.mdx: docs should call out plaintext localStorage token risk and MCPCustomStorage production escape hatch.
examples/with-mcp/server/server.ts: example OAuth server is support evidence for PKCE/DCR/callback flow, not the primary risk surface.
pnpm-lock.yaml and .changeset/react-mcp-initial.md are support material and should stay low-risk unless dependency changes imply runtime risk.
acceptConditions: Accept if docs and examples accurately mirror the runtime review findings.
commentConditions: Comment if docs promise encryption, auto-reconnect, resources/prompts, or production token safety the package does not implement.
